rationale: The cited paper appears in grouped citations as part of general background on factors that affect new car purchases (e.g., dealership locations) rather than as a focal theoretical or methodological basis. It is also cited in a list of prior studies that used or described the PIN data and later in a broad future-research sentence about "when/what to buy" questions. The citing paper does not develop hypotheses from this specific study, reuse its distribution-intensity constructs, or engage its findings directly. Thus, the citation functions as contextual prior-literature mention rather than a substantive building block.
